title: Command-Line Completion
subtitle: {key:Tab} to complete; {key:Ctrl-D} to list.
summary: Tab completes commands, options, filenames, and tags depending on context. {key:Ctrl-D} lists matches. {key:Ctrl-A} inserts ALL matches. The wildmenu makes Tab cycle through choices.

blocks.text: Vim knows what kind of argument the current command expects, and completes accordingly. Tab is context-sensitive: it completes Ex command names, option names, filenames, tag names, and more.

blocks.title: What Gets Completed Where
blocks.text:
Vim infers context from the command:

  • `:e <Tab>` → filenames in cwd.
  • `:set <Tab>` → option names.
  • `:help <Tab>` → help tags.
  • `:colorscheme <Tab>` → installed colorschemes.
  • `:command <Tab>` → user commands.

The `'wildmode'` option controls how Tab behaves:

  • `full` — cycle one match per Tab.
  • `longest` — first complete to longest common prefix.
  • `list` — show all matches without completing.
  • Combined: `set wildmode=longest:full,full` — first Tab completes to longest unique prefix and shows menu, second cycles.

`set wildmenu` enables the *visual* completion menu — a status-line bar of choices that Tab walks through.
`:e `{key:Ctrl-D} is a fast way to list all files in the cwd that match what you've typed so far.
